title: dryopteris granulosa
intro: Dryopteris granulosa, also known as the granular wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to China and Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Dryopteris granulosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. Dryopteris granulosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for internal use, as well as being used topically in the form of poultices or salves for wound healing and skin issues.
